prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: I want to be direct - I'll share my perspective, but I aim to do so thoughtfully and constructively. I think the current trend of "open office plans" is significantly overrated. While they're pitched as fostering collaboration and communication, substantial research suggests they actually reduce face-to-face interaction, increase stress, and decrease productivity. Employees often respond by using noise-canceling headphones and digital communication, which ironically undermines the original collaborative intent. Well-designed spaces with a mix of collaborative and private work areas seem more effective.
